frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
generated_comment: Dei, today in front of the patient attenders sir actually said women on monthly shifts are “less reliable” because of biological reasons. I was standing there after 14 hours duty and felt so humiliated da — if a male doctor is tired it’s hard work, if we are tired suddenly it becomes hormones.
label: non_misogynistic
annotation_note: This is not misogynistic; the speaker is objecting to a sexist remark made by a senior doctor. It encodes workplace discrimination based on menstruation through a first-person reaction that condemns the bias.
persona_text: You are Preethi. Age 28. A Tamil Brahmin woman working as a junior doctor at a private hospital in Chennai. You have been on your feet for 14 hours. You are texting your batchmate from medical college privately after your male senior doctor publicly told a patient's family that the female doctors on monthly shifts might not be as reliable because of biological reasons. You are sharing what happened and how you felt.
